headline: Tesla files permits for first robotaxi-only Supercharger stations in Arizona — dedicated V4 fleet charging hubs not open to public; first physical autonomous fleet infrastructure, sited in Waymo's home market

Q1 2026 earnings call TONIGHT at 5:30 PM ET — Wall Street consensus EPS $0.35 / revenue $22.27B (+15% YoY); 3 bull catalysts: Cybercab ramp rate, robotaxi miles/revenue-per-mile validation, gross margin ex-credits >19%; 50K inventory overhang + energy storage miss are key bear risks; prediction market ~28% beat odds

Tesla lithium refinery in Robstown TX cited in environmental dispute — independent lab finds hexavalent chromium and arsenic in discharge not covered by TCEQ permit; local drainage district issues cease & desist; wastewater flows toward Baffin Bay fishing area

FSD v14.3 MLIR rewrite confirmed: 20% faster reaction times via rebuilt AI compiler; new fleet-to-fleet RL pipeline sourcing 'hard examples' from global fleet; v14.3.1 follow-up patch now rolling fleet-wide on 2026.2.9.7 build

Forbes: Tesla targeting one Cybercab every 10 seconds at full Giga Texas scale — annual capacity of 2 million units; production ramp started April 2026 with Cybercab on AI4.5 hardware (AI5 pushed to 2027)

Optimus Gen 3 enters mass production — Fremont factory floor converted for 1M units/year long-term capacity; GigaShanghai confirmed as second production site; Gen 3 powered by FSD AI; internal factory deployment before external sales

Samsung Taylor Texas 2nm fab kicks off equipment installation April 24 — AI5 chip production milestone; $16.5B Tesla deal signed Jul 2025; 2nm yields in high-50% range; volume production targeting 2027 for Optimus and supercomputer clusters

Dallas/Houston robotaxi fleet expands to 4 unsupervised vehicles after first geofence expansion — early-stage small coverage zones; Tesla committed to full 1H 2026 launches in 7 cities: Dallas, Houston, Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, Las Vegas
